name: T.j. Cinnamon's Cinnamon Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water with 1/2 teaspoon sugar. Let stand for 5 minutes.
2. In a mixer bowl, combine 4 cups flour, remaining 1/3 cup sugar, and salt.
3. Gradually beat in milk, oil, eggs, and yeast mixture; beat until well blended.
4. Beat in additional flour (about 1-1/2 cups) until dough pulls away from the sides of the bowl.
5. Place dough in a greased bowl, turning to grease top. Cover and let rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
6. Filling:
- Beat all filling ingredients together until smooth; set aside.
7. Grease two 9-inch round cake pans.
8. On a lightly floured surface, roll dough into an 18x10-inch rectangle.
9. Spread with filling and roll tightly from the long side.
10. Cut into fourteen 1-1/4 inch slices.
11. Place one roll cut side up in the center of each pan.
12. Arrange remaining rolls in a circle of 6 around the center roll.
13. Cover and let rise until doubled in bulk, about 30 to 40 minutes.
14. Preheat oven to 350°F.
15. Bake 25 to 30 minutes, or until golden brown.
16. Cool in pans for 10 minutes. Invert onto wire racks, then invert again to cool.
Icing:
In a medium bowl, whisk all icing ingredients together until smooth. Drizzle over cooled rolls. (Can be made ahead; wrap well and freeze up to 1 month. Thaw wrapped at room temperature.) Makes 14 rolls. Nutrition per roll: 410 calories, 14 gm fat, 6 gm protein, 67 gm carbohydrates, 250 mg sodium, 60 mg cholesterol. Prep time: 45 minutes plus rising. Baking time: 25 to 30 minutes.
